title: catha rothiana
intro: Catha rothiana, also known as the Roth's Catha or African Khat, is a species of flowering plant in the catha genus, native to the Horn of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, particularly as a stimulant and anxiolytic, due to its high content of cathinone, a natural alkaloid with psychoactive effects. The health benefits of Catha rothiana include improved mental clarity, reduced fatigue, and enhanced focus, although its long-term use has been linked to various health concerns. Catha rothiana is typically consumed as a herbal tea, made by infusing the leaves and young shoots in hot water, although its use is heavily regulated in some countries due to concerns over its potential for abuse.
